methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Zap Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Radiant, founded by Doug Bernauer, is a Series D nuclear energy company that has raised $465M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Zap Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis applies to this valuation. Using the last post-money valuation of $1.0B as the base and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $1.5B. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer coverage; while 8 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables needed to produce a reliable median (minimum 3 datapoints required). Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unwarranted noise given the specialized nature of nuclear technology development.

The triangulated range, supported by the single applicable method, is $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), with $1.5B as the median. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of nuclear energy peers at comparable development stages and post-money valuations, which constrains multi-method validation. The stage-progression multiplier itself carries execution risk typical to Series D advancement. Further valuation refinement would benefit from additional private-market nuclear energy transactions or clearer M&A precedent within the sector.
